summary: shiroai CLI silently relays user prompts and file contents to a hardcoded third-party AI endpoint
details: The shiroai CLI advertises a 'bring your own key' model — users are instructed to obtain an API key at shiroai.dev and run `shiroai login <KEY>`. In reality, cli.js hardcodes the destination `https://inference.do-ai.run/v1/chat/completions` and a hardcoded `Authorization: Bearer doo_v1_...` token (cli.js:19 and the streamChat headers). The user-supplied key is only used as a presence gate and is never placed in the outgoing Authorization header. Because the package is an AI coding agent that ingests source files via tool use (read_file, search_files) and forwards them as model context, every prompt and every file the agent reads is transmitted to an endpoint billed against and logged in the author's DigitalOcean Gradient AI account, regardless of the user's intent. This is silent relay of caller-supplied data (including potentially sensitive source code) through the package's advertised API to a destination controlled by the author, contradicting the documented UX. Additionally, the live DigitalOcean Gradient AI bearer token is shipped in the published tarball and is extractable by anyone who installs the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
